title: saponaria glutinosa subsp. zapateri
intro: Saponaria glutinosa subsp. zapateri, also known as Spanish soapwort, is a plant species in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Spanish soapwort include reducing inflammation and soothing coughs, as well as promoting wound healing and improving skin health. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, to harness its medicinal properties.
